question: Does the paper report HIV sequences from patient samples?
explain: The paper mentions that blood samples were collected from 83 HIV-1 infected but ART naïve individuals, and the HIV-1 RNA was amplified and sequenced. This indicates that the study involved sequencing HIV sequences from patient samples.
sentences: "Blood samples were collected consecutively in 2010 from 83 HIV-1 infected but ART naïve individuals of Gondar University teaching hospital (n = 61), located in the high plateau of northern Ethiopia and of Metema hospital (n = 22), located in northwestern Ethiopia on the border to Sudan."; "Protease (PR) and reverse transcriptase (RT) genes of HIV-1 RNA were amplified and sequenced."

question: Does the paper report in vitro drug susceptibility data?
explain: The paper focuses on the sequencing of HIV-1 RNA from patient samples and the identification of drug resistance mutations. It does not mention any in vitro drug susceptibility testing.
sentences: "Protease (PR) and reverse transcriptase (RT) genes of HIV-1 RNA were amplified and sequenced."; "The frequency of primary DRM to any drug class, using the World Health Organization mutation lists, was 7.2% (6/83), thus exceeding the WHO threshold limit of 5%."

question: What were the GenBank accession numbers for sequenced HIV isolates?
explain: The paper states that the nucleotide sequences analyzed were submitted to GenBank with the accession numbers KJ807649--KJ807731.
sentences: "The nucleotide sequences analysed were submitted to GenBank with the accession numbers KJ807649--KJ807731."

explain: The paper explicitly mentions that the study involved individuals infected with HIV-1.
sentences: "Infection with human immunodeficiency virus type 1 (HIV-1) and acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS) remain a major world-wide health problem."; "In all individuals HIV-1C was found."

question: Which HIV genes were reported to have been sequenced?
explain: The paper mentions that the protease (PR) and reverse transcriptase (RT) genes of HIV-1 RNA were amplified and sequenced.

question: What method was used for sequencing?
explain: The paper describes the use of the fluorescent dideoxy-terminator method (Big Dye Terminator kit; Applied Biosystem) and an automated population-based sequencer (ABI Prism 310), which are indicative of Sanger sequencing.
sentences: "After gel-purification (Wizard SV Gel and PCR Clean-up kit; Promega, Madison, WI), PCR products were sequenced using the fluorescent dideoxy-terminator method (Big Dye Terminator kit; Applied Biosystem, Foster City, CA) using an automated population-based sequencer (ABI Prism 310; Applied Biosystem, Foster City, CA)."

question: Were samples cloned prior to sequencing?
explain: The paper does not mention any cloning of samples prior to sequencing. It describes the direct sequencing of PCR products.
sentences: "After gel-purification (Wizard SV Gel and PCR Clean-up kit; Promega, Madison, WI), PCR products were sequenced using the fluorescent dideoxy-terminator method (Big Dye Terminator kit; Applied Biosystem, Foster City, CA) using an automated population-based sequencer (ABI Prism 310; Applied Biosystem, Foster City, CA)."

question: Did samples undergo single genome sequencing?

question: What clinical specimens were sequenced?
explain: The paper mentions that HIV was diagnosed based on tests for the detection of antibodies in serum, plasma, or whole blood samples, and that samples for HIV viral load determination and nucleic acid sequencing were immediately frozen. This suggests that plasma was used for sequencing.
sentences: "HIV was diagnosed based on agglutination and immunochromatographic tests for the detection of antibodies in serum, plasma or whole blood samples using Determine™, Capillus™, and Uni-Gold™ kits."; "Samples for HIV viral load determination and nucleic acid sequencing were immediately frozen and were kept below −20°C until used."

question: Were any sequences obtained from individuals with virological failure on a treatment regimen?
explain: The study focused on ART naïve individuals, meaning they had not received antiretroviral treatment before, so there would be no sequences from individuals with virological failure on a treatment regimen.
sentences: "The frequency of primary drug resistance mutations (DRMs) and of naturally occurring polymorphisms was determined in 83 antiretroviral treatment (ART) naïve Ethiopian individuals infected with HIV-1, consecutively enrolled in 2010."; "The inclusion criteria for individuals were no history of previous ART, age > 18 years, and no current pregnancy."
